candidate_catalog.summary: Backend API engineer with Python, FastAPI, SQL, Kafka, Docker, and AWS.
Backend engineer with Django, REST APIs, PostgreSQL, and AWS.
Platform-minded engineer with Python automation, Kubernetes, Terraform, and observability.
Data-platform engineer with Python, SQL, Airflow, BigQuery, and ETL.
Balanced full-stack engineer with React, TypeScript, Python, FastAPI, and SQL.
Frontend specialist with React, TypeScript, Jest, Figma, CSS, and design systems.
Frontend product engineer with React, TypeScript, Next.js, Redux, GraphQL, and Jest.
Frontend UI engineer with Vue, JavaScript, CSS, Figma, and Storybook.
TypeScript-heavy mobile frontend engineer using React Native and GraphQL.
Quality engineer specializing in Playwright, Cypress, Jest, and TypeScript.
ML engineer with Python, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Pandas, OpenCV, and production modeling.
Applied ML engineer using Python, SQL, Pandas, XGBoost, and experimentation pipelines.
MLOps profile with Python, TensorFlow, Airflow, Kubernetes, GCP, and production pipelines.
Data engineer with Python, SQL, Airflow, Spark, BigQuery, and warehouse operations.
Analytics engineer with SQL, dbt, Python, and semantic modeling.
Low-fit generalist with spreadsheet, support, and WordPress work.
Junior web profile with JavaScript, HTML, CSS, and light Node.js exposure.
Backend-platform engineer with Python, Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, AWS, and SQL.
Backend engineer with Python, FastAPI, Redis, Kafka, PostgreSQL, and REST APIs.
ML engineer with Python, PyTorch, transformers, NLP, and evaluation workflows.

jobs.label: backend-api-observability
jobs.notes: FastAPI, REST, SQL, and production service operations matter most. Kafka or platform depth should help, while clearly non-backend profiles should fall to the bottom.

judgments.reason: Best direct overlap across Python, FastAPI, SQL, Kafka, Docker, and AWS with clear production API project evidence.
Very strong backend overlap and eventing depth, but slightly narrower cloud-platform evidence than candidate 01.
Excellent infrastructure and deployment match for observability-heavy backend work, though less API-specific than candidates 01 and 19.
No meaningful software-engineering or API background.
Too junior and too shallow for backend systems and observability ownership.
Strong engineer, but skill profile is frontend-design-system work rather than backend API infrastructure.

jobs.label: frontend-react-typescript
jobs.notes: React and TypeScript should dominate. Design-system and testing depth deserve extra credit, while non-frontend specialists should stay clearly below the real UI candidates.

judgments.reason: Most directly aligned profile with React, TypeScript, Jest, Figma, and design-system work in a senior-looking package.
Excellent React and TypeScript overlap, though more product-feature oriented and slightly less design-system heavy than candidate 06.
Full-stack candidate should still rate well because of strong React and TypeScript credibility, but not above the frontend specialists.
Strong ML background, but wrong domain and skill mix for a React lead role.
Good backend engineer, but lacks the React and TypeScript focus the role centers on.

jobs.label: ml-platform-engineer
jobs.notes: The role blends model work and production ML operations. TensorFlow plus pipeline infrastructure should beat pure research or pure data candidates, while clearly non-ML profiles should sink.

judgments.reason: Strongest blend of TensorFlow, Python, orchestration, cloud, and production ML pipeline maintenance.
Deep ML credibility and TensorFlow fit, though less directly aligned with orchestration and GCP-heavy pipeline operations.
Strong applied-ML and evaluation background, but weaker direct overlap with TensorFlow and infrastructure tooling.
Entry-level web profile without machine-learning or pipeline experience.
Frontend UI specialist, not an ML or data-pipeline profile.
